Furthermore, since the weights occupy more than 1.5 TB of HBM capacity, the KV cache for K3’s KDA and Gated MLA will need to be offloaded to CPU DDR5 and NVMe, even at relatively low user concurrency, because little space remains in HBM. 6/8

The unfortunate downside of the WideEP optimization is that it consumes a tremendous amount of network bandwidth. WideEP is highly optimized for rack-scale systems like the GB200/GB300 NVL72, whose copper backplane provides 18× more bandwidth than comparable DGX B200 systems.
WideEP distributes the 896 experts across many GPUs so that each GPU’s HBM contains only a small number of experts, optimizing per-token memory usage and compute utilization. 4/8

Secondly, although Kimi Delta Attention has up to 10× lower networking requirements for KV-cache transfers, its large weights require even more network bandwidth to implement an optimization called WideEP, which spreads the weights across different GPUs. 3/8

Kimi K3 is actually quite positive for NVIDIA, as large-model inference is where the NVL72 shines. Because K3 has more than 2.8 trillion parameters, it requires a large scale-up domain to store its weights. 2/8
